sections.paragraph: οὔτε [so accented because it is really two words οὐ, τέ] neg. particle dismissing an activity or thing that follows the particle, and most often coupled formula-ically with another οὔτε or functioning in similar fashion neither... nor Mt 6:20; 12:32; 22:30; Mk 14:68; Lk 12:24; J 4:21; Ac 2:31; 24:12; Ro 8:38f; 1 Cor 6:9f; 11:11; Gal 1:12; 1 Th 2:5f; Rv 3:15f; 9:20. Cp. οὐδείς... οὔτε no one... or 5:4; οὐ... οὔτε not... nor 9:21. —Occasionally a question or clause preceding a single οὔτε functions as though it were also introduced by οὔτε J 4:11; Js 3:12; 3 J 10.
